marketState: neutral - fairly priced, evidence genuinely split between bullish weather and bearish storage/technicals
summary: Front price ($2.927) sits within a few cents of fair value at every horizon out to +21D (all statuses FAIR), so there is no valuation-based edge right now. Seasonality shows price running below its typical seasonal path (-7.2pp vs median), but the dashboard itself flags this signal's reliability as Low this week (59pp 10-90% spread), so it should be discounted. Weather demand is genuinely above 5-year normal in both the 1-7D (+1.9%) and especially 8-16D (+9.7%) windows, a real bullish undertone, even though the 24h demand trend just cooled. Working against that: storage surplus keeps widening (+180 Bcf vs 5y, latest build printed a bearish +22 Bcf surprise vs forecast) and the daily/weekly technicals are in a clean bearish trend (50<200 EMA, negative MACD), while intraday (5m/15m) shows only a minor bounce. Managed-money is near a 5-year-low net-short percentile (8th), a crowded short that raises squeeze risk for anyone chasing the bearish storage/technical case.

longTerm.rationale: Top-priority signals conflict: weather demand is solidly above normal (+1.9%/+9.7%), but seasonality's own reliability is flagged Low and price is already fairly valued (spread under $0.09) at every horizon, so there's no mispricing to lean on. Storage surplus (+180 Bcf vs 5y, bearish weekly surprise) argues for downside, but managed money is already near a 5y-extreme net-short (8th percentile), making fresh shorts vulnerable to a squeeze if weather stays hot.
longTerm.invalidation: A confirmed close below ~$2.70 (storage/seasonality bearish case playing out) or above ~$3.10-3.15 with daily trend flipping bullish (weather-driven demand case playing out) would resolve the conflict and justify a directional trade.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for either a sustained break below ~$2.70 (aligns with storage surplus + weak seasonality, favors SHORT) or a daily trend/EMA flip back to bullish with demand staying >5% above 5y normal (favors LONG).

shortTerm.rationale: Front price is essentially exactly at fair value (spread +$0.004, FAIR), leaving no near-term valuation edge. Technicals disagree by timeframe - 5m/15m show a mild bullish bounce (EMA 50>200, RSI ~60, MACD positive) while the more decision-relevant 1h/4h are neutral and 1d/1w remain bearish (death cross, negative MACD). The small front/next backwardation (-$0.026) and modest near-term weather demand (+1.9% vs 5y) aren't strong enough to force a breakout call either way.
shortTerm.invalidation: A close above ~$2.97 (next contract's current level, would confirm the intraday bounce) or below ~$2.85 (would confirm the daily/weekly bearish trend taking over) would give a clean directional signal.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for 1h/4h trend to align with either the bullish 5m/15m bounce (break/close above ~$2.97 for a long) or the bearish daily/weekly trend reasserting with a close below ~$2.85 (for a short).

keyDrivers: Weather demand above 5y normal in both 1-7D (+1.9%) and 8-16D (+9.7%) windows
Price sits within $0.004-$0.111 of fair value at every horizon (now through +21D), all flagged FAIR
Storage surplus widening: +180 Bcf vs 5y, latest weekly build surprised bearish (+22 Bcf vs forecast)
Managed money net short near a 5y extreme (8th percentile) - crowded short, squeeze risk for new shorts
Daily/weekly technicals bearish (50<200 EMA, negative MACD) vs a mild bullish bounce on intraday charts
risks: Seasonality reliability is explicitly flagged Low this week (59pp 10-90% spread), so its bearish tilt shouldn't be trusted much
Extreme net-short COT positioning can unwind violently on any hot-weather surprise, punishing fresh short entries
Fair-value band is wide (~$1/MMBtu), so 'FAIR' status tolerates large price swings without producing a real valuation signal
disclaimer: Informational only, not financial advice. Auto-generated hourly.
